title: Projects
text:
This page highlights academic and professional projects related to marketing, analytics, dashboards, presentations, and data-driven decision-making.



Focus: Data science concepts and technical reflection
Tools: R, RStudio, Quarto
In this project, I wrote a reflection essay on my experience learning the basics of data science using R, Quarto, and RStudio. The assignment helped me understand how these tools work together to analyze data and present information in a clear and organized way. I also learned how Quarto can combine code, text, and output into one professional document, which is useful for communicating insights.
Skills shown: R basics, Quarto document creation, data analysis concepts, technical writing Open full reflection essay



Focus: Survey research and data analysis
Tools: Quarto, survey questionnaire, research design
This project focused on developing research questions and identifying independent and dependent variables based on an existing survey about students’ research participation experience. In addition, I worked with my group to organize and analyze the data given to us. This project helped me better understand how survey responses can be used to draw meaningful insights.
Skills shown: Research design, survey interpretation, data wrangling, data visualization, Quarto presentation Open full group project



Focus: Data analysis and visualization using R
Tools: R, RStudio, tidyverse, Quarto
In this assignment, I applied data science concepts using R to solve a series of problems involving data manipulation, conditional logic, and visualization. I worked with real datasets to clean, organize, and analyze data, and created visualizations to communicate insights. The project also involved building reusable functions and using tools like the tidyverse to streamline analysis.
Skills shown: R programming, data wrangling, data visualization, functional programming, Quarto presentation, problem-solving Open full R application project

title: Projects
text:
Focus: Data analysis and dashboard reporting
Tools: BigQuery, Looker Studio, Google Cloud

Skills shown: SQL basics, data visualization, dashboard design, reporting

title: Dashboard
text:
This dashboard presents an example of a data visualization created in R using student-teacher ratio data from my M11-2 Data Analysis Project.



The chart below compares countries with the highest and lowest student-teacher ratios. This helps show differences in education systems and class-size patterns across countries.













Countries with higher student-teacher ratios may have larger class sizes.
Countries with lower ratios may have more individualized student attention.
Data visualization makes it easier to compare education patterns across countries.




R and the tidyverse were used to clean and organize the dataset, allowing for easier analysis. Quarto was used to present the results and visualization in a clear and structured format.

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
Savannah Ventic

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Why Consumer Behavior Matters
Consumer behavior helps marketers understand why people buy, what influences them, and how they respond to advertising.

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Key Factors

Motivation
Perception
Social influence
Brand attitudes
Online behavior

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Advertising Strategy
Effective advertising uses consumer insights to create messages that connect with the right audience.

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Example
A brand targeting Gen Z consumers may use social media trends, influencer partnerships, and short-form video to increase engagement.

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Career Connection
Understanding consumer behavior can help me prepare for roles in advertising, promotional marketing, and marketing strategy.

title: Consumer Behavior and Advertising Strategy
text:
Conclusion
Consumer behavior is important because it helps marketers create campaigns that are more relevant, persuasive, and effective.

text:

title: Analytics
text: This page explains how website analytics can be used to understand visitor behavior and improve a professional portfolio website. The main tools used for this process include Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Google BigQuery, and Looker Studio.

title: Analytics
text: This page explains how website analytics can be used to understand visitor behavior and improve a professional portfolio website. The main tools used for this process include Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Google BigQuery, and Looker Studio.

title: Analytics
text:
Google Analytics
Google Analytics can track how visitors interact with the website. Important metrics include page views, users, engagement time, traffic sources, and conversions. These metrics help show which pages attract the most attention and how visitors move through the website.

title: Analytics
text:
Google Search Console
Google Search Console helps track how the website appears in Google Search. It can show search impressions, clicks, average position, and search queries. This information is useful for improving search engine optimization and making the website easier to find online.

title: Analytics
text:
BigQuery
Google BigQuery can be used to store and analyze Google Analytics data. This allows more advanced analysis using SQL. With BigQuery, website traffic data can be cleaned, filtered, and summarized for deeper insights.

title: Analytics
text:
Looker Studio
Looker Studio can be used to create dashboards that visualize website performance. Example dashboard metrics include total users, page views, traffic source, most visited pages, and conversion activity.

title: Analytics
text:
Insights
Based on these analytics tools, I would evaluate which pages receive the most traffic, how visitors find the website, and whether visitors are engaging with the portfolio content. These insights could help improve the website design, project descriptions, and search visibility.

title: Analytics
text:
Conclusion
Using analytics tools can help turn website activity into useful business insights. For a professional portfolio website, this information can support better content decisions, stronger personal branding, and improved visibility to potential employers.
